subject: The win streak nobody warns you about

content.subject: The win streak nobody warns you about
content.marketRecap: Monday's London open did exactly what smart money loves to do — probe Friday's range extremes before committing to a direction, shaking out reactive traders on both sides. With GDP on deck this week, institutions are likely in positioning mode rather than trending mode, which means choppy price delivery and a lot of engineered liquidity above and below key weekly levels. ICT traders should be treating this as a read-and-wait environment, not a chase-every-setup environment.

articles.title: Why Discipline Breaks Down After Winning Streaks
articles.excerpt: Everyone warns about revenge trading after losses. Nobody talks about the invisible overconfidence that follows 3-5 consecutive wins — and how it silently wrecks funded accounts built on smart money concepts.

articles.title: The GDP Trade: Why I Do Nothing
articles.excerpt: After 10+ years of watching GDP releases wreck otherwise disciplined traders, I've learned the most profitable move is a structured, rules-based decision to sit out — and why that's harder than any entry you'll ever take.

articles.title: The Quiet Session That Rewired My Trading Brain
articles.excerpt: Most psychology content tells you to control your emotions. I'm arguing the opposite — that one deliberately boring, no-trade Asian session observed weekly as a ritual is the single most underrated psychological training tool in ICT trading.

videoOfTheWeek.title: The Biggest GDP Trading Myth (That's Costing You Money in 2026)

content.tipOfTheWeek: Ahead of a high-impact GDP release, mark your nearest unmitigated order blocks on the 1-hour chart and treat them as decision zones — not entry triggers. If price sweeps a liquidity pool and taps into a valid order block inside a killzone (London or New York open), that confluence is your signal to engage. If those conditions aren't all present simultaneously, the most disciplined move is no move at all.
content.comingUp: GDP hits this week and it is the highest-impact macro event of the quarter — quarterly GDP surprises have historically triggered sharp trend reversals that wrong-foot traders who enter impulsively in the first 90 seconds. The play is preparation, not prediction: identify your key fair value gaps and liquidity levels now, define your no-trade rules before the number drops, and let the dust settle before any smart money concepts setup is even considered valid.
